𝗔𝗕𝗧𝗢𝗣 | Monitoring and managing the performance and resource utilization of multiple AI coding agent sessions in a terminal environment.  🔥 Super Hot!

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Monitoring and managing the performance and resource utilization of multiple AI coding agent sessions in a terminal environment.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: Jun 2026

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Real-time monitoring of AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenCode)
✅ Tracks token usage, context window percentage, and rate limits
✅ Detects open ports and child processes spawned by agents
✅ Supports multiple active agent sessions across macOS, Linux, and Windows
✅ Terminal User Interface (TUI) with themes and customizable display
✅ Optional JSON output for scripting and integration
✅ tmux integration for session jumping


❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2.9K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 282 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 12 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

abtop is a terminal-based monitoring tool for AI coding agents, inspired by htop. It provides real-time insights into the performance and resource usage of agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, and OpenCode, displaying token consumption, rate limits, context window status, and active network ports.

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/graykode/abtop

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗗𝗢𝗖𝗞𝗘𝗥-𝗔𝗡𝗗𝗥𝗢𝗜𝗗 | Running Android emulators in a Docker container for automated testing and CI/CD pipelines.  🔥 Super Hot!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 Shell 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Running Android emulators in a Docker container for automated testing and CI/CD pipelines.
📅 Created: Feb 2023 • Updated: Jun 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Minimal Alpine based image with Android emulator and KVM support
✅ Bundles Java Runtime Environment 11
✅ Customizable Android version, device type, and image types
✅ Port-forwarding of emulator and ADB
✅ Emulator images are wiped on restart
✅ Headless execution suitable for CI farms
✅ Compatible with scrcpy for remote control


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 6K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 448 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 13 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

This repository provides a minimal and customizable Docker image for running the Android emulator as a service. It's designed for headless execution, making it ideal for CI/CD pipelines and automated testing environments.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/HQarroum/docker-android

𝗠𝗜𝗥𝗢𝗦𝗛𝗔𝗥𝗞 | Simulating and forecasting outcomes of various scenarios using swarm intelligence agents.  ✨ Notable!

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Simulating and forecasting outcomes of various scenarios using swarm intelligence agents.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: Jun 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Simulate anything from press releases to policy drafts
✅ Hundreds of grounded AI agents (Twitter, Reddit, prediction market)
✅ Hour-by-hour simulation progression
✅ Interactive simulation: chat with agents, drop breaking news, fork timelines
✅ Generates reports with cited posts and trades
✅ Quick start with OpenRouter key and launcher
✅ Multiple installation paths (Cloud deploy, Docker, local Ollama, Claude Code CLI)


⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

🍴 270 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

MiroShark is a universal swarm intelligence engine that simulates complex scenarios by spawning hundreds of AI agents. These agents interact within a simulated environment, posting, arguing, and trading based on an initial input, providing insights and reports on the simulated outcomes.

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/aaronjmars/MiroShark

𝗗𝗘𝗩𝗙𝗜𝗡𝗗 | To serve as a discoverable directory for developers to showcase their skills and find collaborators or employment opportunities.  🔍 Worth a Look

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To serve as a discoverable directory for developers to showcase their skills and find collaborators or employment opportunities.
📅 Created: Apr 2023 • Updated: Jun 2026

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Search functionality for developers based on skills, location, or name
✅ Platform for developers to showcase skills and connect with collaborators
✅ User-friendly and searchable format for discovering developers
✅ Developer profiles created in JSON format


┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

devFind is an open-source platform designed for developers to create profiles showcasing their skills and connect with potential collaborators. Profiles are created in JSON format and are discoverable through a user-friendly, searchable web interface.

✅ Reusable Datepicker component for React
✅ Supports date and time selection
✅ Localization support
✅ Custom header configuration
✅ Integration with date-fns and moment.js
✅ Configurable time intervals
✅ Click outside handler

React Date Picker is a versatile and reusable date selection component for React applications. It offers extensive customization options, including time selection, localization, and custom header support, making it easy to integrate date and time picking functionality into your UI.
